cards.title: Integrative Pain Management — Framework

alerts.label: Integrative Approach to Pain
alerts.text: Pain is not merely a sensory experience — it is a multidimensional phenomenon encompassing sensory-discriminative, affective-motivational, and cognitive-evaluative components (the biopsychosocial model). Integrative pain management combines evidence-based naturopathic modalities with conventional medical care to address all dimensions of pain, reduce opioid dependency, and restore function and quality of life.

rows: Well-localised, proportional to injury, inflammatory signs
Manual therapy, hydrotherapy, anti-inflammatory nutrition, acupuncture
Poorly localised, referred, colicky/cramping, autonomic features
Abdominal hydrotherapy, yoga (parasympathetic), acupuncture, dietary modification
Alpha-lipoic acid, B12, acupuncture (GB34, ST36), TENS, mindfulness
Widespread pain, disproportionate to stimulus, fatigue, cognitive fog
Graded exercise, mindfulness, pain neuroscience education (PNE), sleep optimisation
Fibromyalgia, IBS, tension headache — pain with normal imaging
Mindfulness, yoga, exercise, psychological support, sleep, nutrition

cards._legacyHtml:
Integrative Approach to Pain
Pain is not merely a sensory experience — it is a multidimensional phenomenon encompassing sensory-discriminative, affective-motivational, and cognitive-evaluative components (the biopsychosocial model). Integrative pain management combines evidence-based naturopathic modalities with conventional medical care to address all dimensions of pain, reduce opioid dependency, and restore function and quality of life.
Pain TypeMechanismKey FeaturesPrimary Integrative Approach
Nociceptive (Somatic)Tissue damage activating nociceptorsWell-localised, proportional to injury, inflammatory signsManual therapy, hydrotherapy, anti-inflammatory nutrition, acupuncture
Nociceptive (Visceral)Visceral nociceptors — stretch, ischaemiaPoorly localised, referred, colicky/cramping, autonomic featuresAbdominal hydrotherapy, yoga (parasympathetic), acupuncture, dietary modification
NeuropathicNerve damage / central sensitisationBurning, shooting, dysaesthesia, allodynia, hyperalgesiaAlpha-lipoic acid, B12, acupuncture (GB34, ST36), TENS, mindfulness
Central SensitisationAmplified central processing — wind-upWidespread pain, disproportionate to stimulus, fatigue, cognitive fogGraded exercise, mindfulness, pain neuroscience education (PNE), sleep optimisation
NociplasticAltered nociception without tissue/nerve damageFibromyalgia, IBS, tension headache — pain with normal imagingMindfulness, yoga, exercise, psychological support, sleep, nutrition

cards.title: Naturopathic Pain Assessment

steps.title: Pain History (SOCRATES)
steps.text: Site, Onset, Character (burning/stabbing/aching/cramping), Radiation, Alleviating factors, Timing (constant/intermittent, worse AM/PM), Exacerbating factors, Severity (NRS 0–10). Additionally: pain catastrophising (PCS), sleep impact, mood impact, function impact (how many activities limited?), social impact. Never underestimate pain — it is always real to the patient.

steps.title: Red Flag Screen — Always
steps.text: Night pain (waking from sleep — bone tumour, infection), weight loss + pain (malignancy), fever + pain (infection), pain after trauma (fracture), neurological symptoms (cord compression), bowel/bladder dysfunction (cauda equina), pain in immunocompromised (opportunistic infection). ANY red flag = REFER before naturopathic management.

steps.title: Naturopathic Pain Assessment
steps.text: Assess: dietary inflammatory load (pro-inflammatory diet perpetuates pain), sleep quality (sleep deprivation amplifies pain — insomnia → central sensitisation), stress and cortisol (chronic stress → ↑ substance P, ↓ pain tolerance), gut health (gut-brain-pain axis: dysbiosis correlates with fibromyalgia, IBS-related pain), movement patterns (kinesiophobia, pain catastrophising), constitutional assessment (Prakriti: Vata excess = neurological/neuropathic type pain).

steps.title: Validated Pain Tools
steps.text: NRS (Numerical Rating Scale 0–10): simple, reliable, use at every visit. BPI (Brief Pain Inventory): severity + interference. DN4 (Douleur Neuropathique 4): screen for neuropathic component (≥4/10 = neuropathic). PSQI (Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index): sleep and pain are bidirectional. PHQ-9 + GAD-7: depression and anxiety amplify pain 2–3×. PSEQ (Pain Self-Efficacy Questionnaire): predicts treatment response.

cards._legacyHtml:
1
Pain History (SOCRATES) Site, Onset, Character (burning/stabbing/aching/cramping), Radiation, Alleviating factors, Timing (constant/intermittent, worse AM/PM), Exacerbating factors, Severity (NRS 0–10). Additionally: pain catastrophising (PCS), sleep impact, mood impact, function impact (how many activities limited?), social impact. Never underestimate pain — it is always real to the patient.

ANY red flag = REFER before naturopathic management.

Validated Pain Tools NRS (Numerical Rating Scale 0–10): simple, reliable, use at every visit. BPI (Brief Pain Inventory): severity + interference. DN4 (Douleur Neuropathique 4): screen for neuropathic component (≥4/10 = neuropathic). PSQI (Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index): sleep and pain are bidirectional. PHQ-9 + GAD-7: depression and anxiety amplify pain 2–3×. PSEQ (Pain Self-Efficacy Questionnaire): predicts treatment response.

cards.title: Integrative Pain Protocols by Condition

rows: Acupuncture ★★★, spinal manipulation ★★★, yoga ★★★, TENS ★★, hydrotherapy (contrast)
Curcumin 500mg TID, omega-3 3g/day, magnesium glycinate 400mg, Vit D 2000IU. Eliminate: refined carbs, trans fats, alcohol
Acupuncture ★★★, hydrotherapy (warm baths), manipulation/mobilisation ★★★, tai chi ★★
Glucosamine 1500mg/day, chondroitin 1200mg/day (modest benefit), omega-3, collagen peptides 10g/day, cherry extract (gout)
Aerobic exercise ★★★ (graded), mindfulness ★★, acupuncture ★★, hydrotherapy ★★, CBT ★★★
Malic acid + magnesium, CoQ10, SAMe 800mg/day, Vit D, optimise sleep (5-HTP 100–300mg before bed), eliminate nightshades
Magnesium glycinate 400–600mg/day ★★★, riboflavin 400mg/day ★★, CoQ10 300mg/day ★★, butterbur 75mg BD ★★★ (liver toxicity risk — use PA-free extract), feverfew 100mg/day
Alpha-lipoic acid 600mg/day ★★★ (diabetic neuropathy), B12 methylcobalamin 1000mcg/day, Vit D 4000IU, acetyl-L-carnitine 1–2g/day, NAC 600mg BD
Omega-3 3g/day ★★ (comparable to ibuprofen), magnesium 300mg/day, ginger 250mg QID ★★, zinc 30mg/day, Vit D, reduce refined sugar + dairy

cards.title: Pain Neuroscience Education (PNE) — Patient Communication

alerts.label: Why PNE Matters
alerts.text: Pain education is a treatment, not just information. Explaining how pain works — that it is a protective output of the brain, not always a sign of tissue damage — reduces pain catastrophising, kinesiophobia (fear of movement), and hypervigilance. PNE alone reduces chronic pain scores by 20–30% before any other intervention.

pearls.label: 🟡 Opioid Harm Reduction in Integrative Practice
pearls.text: Many patients presenting to BNYS practitioners are on or reducing opioid medication. Key points: Never advise stopping prescribed opioids abruptly — withdrawal is dangerous. Work alongside prescribing physician. Naturopathic interventions that reduce opioid requirements: acupuncture (reduces opioid use post-surgery ★★★), mindfulness (reduces opioid craving and catastrophising), exercise (endogenous opioid upregulation), omega-3 + curcumin (anti-inflammatory → reduced nociception). Document reductions in pain scores and function — evidence to support prescriber's tapering decisions.

steps.title: Key Messages for Patients
steps.text: "Pain does not equal damage." Pain is the brain's alarm system — in chronic pain, the alarm becomes hypersensitive (like a smoke detector going off when there's no fire). Hurt does not mean harm. Moving is safe and necessary for healing. Sleep, stress, and thoughts all influence pain — this is biology, not imagination. Recovery is possible and common.

steps.title: Graded Activity & Graded Exposure
steps.text: For kinesiophobia: identify feared activities. Rate fear (0–10). Begin with least feared (2–3/10). Progress only when fear reduces. For deconditioning: baseline activity level → 10% increase per week. Never push through severe pain — stay below 4/10 NRS. Flare-up plan: return to previous tolerated level, not complete rest.

steps.title: Sleep & Pain Bidirectional Relationship
steps.text: Poor sleep → ↑ pain sensitivity (reduced pain threshold by 25%). Pain → poor sleep → more pain. Break cycle: CBT-I (cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia) reduces pain intensity independent of sleep improvement. Naturopathic sleep support: magnesium, 5-HTP, lavender aromatherapy, sleep hygiene protocol, yoga Nidra.

cards.title: Integrative Pain Management — Supplement Protocol

rows: ★★★ Equivalent to ibuprofen for knee OA pain
Anticoagulant interaction (high dose). Reduce gall bladder disease (stimulates contraction)
Competitive inhibitor of arachidonic acid → ↓ PGE2, LTB4
Anticoagulants (monitor INR at doses >3g). Fishy aftertaste — take with meals
NMDA receptor antagonism — blocks central sensitisation. Muscle relaxation
★★★ Migraine prophylaxis, fibromyalgia, dysmenorrhoea, tension headache
Renal failure (accumulates). Diarrhoea with oxide/citrate forms — glycinate better tolerated
Antioxidant — reduces oxidative stress in neural tissue, regenerates glutathione
Hypoglycaemia (potentiates insulin). Biotin depletion with long-term use — supplement biotin
GI upset. Drug interaction: statins, anticoagulants. Avoid in pregnancy
Modulates immune response, reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines, VDR in neural tissue
★★★ Deficiency associated with fibromyalgia, LBP, widespread musculoskeletal pain
Monitor 25(OH)D. Toxicity if >10,000IU/day sustained. Hypercalcaemia risk

cards._legacyHtml:
SupplementDoseMechanismEvidenceCautions
Curcumin (Bioperine complex)500mg TID with foodNF-κB inhibition, COX-2 inhibition, TNF-α ↓★★★ Equivalent to ibuprofen for knee OA painAnticoagulant interaction (high dose). Reduce gall bladder disease (stimulates contraction)
Omega-3 (EPA+DHA)3–4g/day EPA+DHA combinedCompetitive inhibitor of arachidonic acid → ↓ PGE2, LTB4★★★ RA, LBP, dysmenorrhoea, migraine prophylaxisAnticoagulants (monitor INR at doses >3g). Fishy aftertaste — take with meals
Magnesium Glycinate300–600mg elemental/dayNMDA receptor antagonism — blocks central sensitisation. Muscle relaxation★★★ Migraine prophylaxis, fibromyalgia, dysmenorrhoea, tension headacheRenal failure (accumulates). Diarrhoea with oxide/citrate forms — glycinate better tolerated
Alpha-lipoic Acid600mg/dayAntioxidant — reduces oxidative stress in neural tissue, regenerates glutathione★★★ Diabetic peripheral neuropathy pain (Cochrane review)Hypoglycaemia (potentiates insulin). Biotin depletion with long-term use — supplement biotin
Boswellia Serrata400mg TID (65% boswellic acids)5-LOX inhibition → ↓ leukotriene synthesis → ↓ joint inflammation★★★ Knee OA, RA, IBD-related painGI upset. Drug interaction: statins, anticoagulants. Avoid in pregnancy
Vitamin D32000–4000 IU/day (or per serum levels)Modulates immune response, reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines, VDR in neural tissue★★★ Deficiency associated with fibromyalgia, LBP, widespread musculoskeletal painMonitor 25(OH)D. Toxicity if >10,000IU/day sustained. Hypercalcaemia risk
